senses.definition: Relating to or affecting the two parietal bones of the skull.
examples: The injury affected the biparietal region of the skull.
Biparietal measurements were taken during the anatomical review.

senses.definition: Having two births; producing offspring in two separate birth events.
examples: Some species are biparous, giving birth to two offspring at once.
In mammals, biparous reproduction is common in many species.

senses.definition: Having two parts or segments; divided into two parts.
examples: The biparted structure consists of two distinct sections.
A biparted route divides at the central junction.

senses.definition: Capable of being divided into two parts.
examples: The bipartible material can be split along a seam.
A bipartible design allows modular assembly.

senses.definition: Having two parts or being split into two parts.
examples: The bipartient sections were labeled A and B.
A bipartient approach considers two complementary factors.

senses.definition: Divisible into two equal parts.
examples: The bipartile plot was evenly divided.
A bipartile dataset splits into two halves for validation.

senses.definition: Relating to or supported by two parties or political groups.
examples: A bipartisan bill was proposed to address the issue.
The committee sought bipartisan support for the reform.

senses.definition: The principle or practice of mutual collaboration between two political parties.
examples: Bipartisanism is often valued for passing broad reforms.
The party leaders discussed policies with a spirit of bipartisanism.

senses.definition: Plural form of bipartisanism; multiple instances of cross-party collaboration.
examples: Scholars discussed various bipartisanisms in recent legislation.
The report notes several bipartisanisms across committees.

senses.definition: The practice of two political parties working together to pass laws or address issues.
examples: Bipartisanship helped pass the education reform.
There is a call for more bipartisanship in the cabinet.

senses.definition: Multiple instances or examples of bipartisanship.
examples: The report highlights several bipartisanships achieved this year.
Historically, such bipartisanships are rare.

senses.definition: Divided into two parts; having two distinct components.
examples: A bipartite graph has two sets of vertices.
The bipartite agreement split responsibilities between parties.

senses.definition: In a bipartite or two-part manner.
examples: The task was bipartitely organized into two sections.
The dataset was bipartitely arranged for analysis.

senses.definition: The act or result of dividing something into two parts.
examples: The bipartition of the tour route split it into north and south legs.
A bipartition separates the dataset into two groups.

senses.definition: Multiple instances of dividing into two parts; two-way splits.
examples: The algorithm uses several bipartitions to test stability.
Different bipartitions yield different clustering results.

senses.definition: Misspelling or variant of bipartisan; relating to two political parties.
examples: A bipartizan effort was reported in the press.
The clause aimed for bipartizan support.

senses.definition: A political party composed of two main groups; a two-party system.
examples: The country shifted toward a strong biparty.
Biparty politics dominated the election discourse.

senses.definition: Relating to or occurring around the Paschal (Easter) season; of or relating to Easter.
examples: Bipaschal rituals occur during the liturgical season.
Bipaschal observances mark the period after Passover in some calendars.

senses.definition: Having two comb-like projections or processes on each side; bilaterally feathered or arranged in a two-branched pattern.
examples: Bipectinate antennae are found in some insects.
The bipectinate structure increases surface area.

senses.definition: Having two comb-like projections on each side.
examples: Bipectinated wings helped the insect sense air currents.
The bipectinated appendages are notable for their symmetry.

senses.definition: An animal that uses two legs to walk; a two-legged creature.
examples: Humans are bipeds.
Many birds are bipeds when walking on land.

senses.definition: Relating to or using two legs for walking.
examples: Humans are bipedal by evolution.
Bipedal locomotion frees the hands for other tasks.

senses.definition: The condition of using two legs to walk; the mode of locomotion of two-legged animals.
examples: Bipedalism is a key feature of human evolution.
The fossil shows evidence of bipedalism.

senses.definition: Multiple forms or instances of bipedalism; occurrences of two-legged locomotion.
examples: Different species exhibit various bipedalisms.
The study compares several bipedalisms across taxa.

senses.definition: The states or forms of being bipedal; instances of two-legged walking.
examples: Fossils show various bipedalities in early hominins.
The article discusses the evolution of bipedalities.
